Document:
# Surgery Surgery is hands-on medical work: stitching up injuries and pulling out (or putting in) organs. You need a patient lying down, a drape, and the right tools.
## Requirements To begin surgery, you need: - A [color=#a4885c]patient lying down[/color] (you can't operate on standing patients) - A [color=#a4885c]surgical drape[/color] or [color=#a4885c]bedsheet[/color] to drape the patient - The correct [color=#a4885c]surgical tools[/color] for each step
You [color=#993333]cannot perform surgery on yourself[/color].
## Surgical Tools
## Tool Tiers Better tools mean faster work:
- [color=#a4885c]Improvised[/color] (no tier) - 1.5x duration - [color=#a4885c]Standard[/color] - normal speed (1.0x) - [color=#a4885c]Advanced[/color] (e.g. advanced scalpel, electric saw) - 0.8x duration - [color=#a4885c]Experimental[/color] (e.g. laser scalpel, advanced saw) - 0.7x duration
## Draping Use a [color=#a4885c]surgical drape[/color] or [color=#a4885c]bedsheet[/color] on a patient to open the procedure menu. Surgical drapes work at normal speed (1.0x). Bedsheets work in a pinch but take [color=#993333]1.5x as long[/color].
You can find surgical drapes in medical vendors, medical crates, or craft them at a lathe.
## Surgery Surfaces Buckle your patient to a surgery surface before starting and things go faster:
Operating on the [color=#993333]floor without a surface takes 2.0x as long[/color].
## Performing Steps Each procedure is a sequence of tool steps. Use the [color=#a4885c]right tool[/color] on the draped patient to advance. Wrong tool gives a warning popup.
How long each step takes depends on: [color=#a4885c]base time x surface x drape x tool tier[/color]. A doctor with experimental tools on an operating table with a surgical drape finishes way faster than someone improvising on the floor with a bedsheet.
When all steps are done, use a [color=#a4885c]cautery[/color] to close the incision and finish. The cautery stops bleeding and removes the drape.
## Surgical Tray
The surgical tray is a wheeled cart for organizing your tools. It shows each tool you store inside as a sprite. Fold it to carry, unfold it to wheel around. Drag-drop it onto yourself to fold and pick it up.
## Procedures
### Tend Wounds (Brute) Repairs physical damage (blunt, slash, piercing). - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - treat wounds (repeats automatically until healed) - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
### Tend Wounds (Burn) Treats burn damage (heat, shock, cold, caustic). - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - treat burns (repeats automatically until healed) - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
### Organ Manipulation Open the body cavity to remove or insert organs. - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - clamp vessels - [color=#a4885c]Bone saw[/color] - saw through bone - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - select organ to remove (opens organ picker), or use an organ in your hand on the patient to insert it - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
## Cancelling Surgery The patient can click the [color=#a4885c]drape alert[/color] on their HUD to remove the drape and cancel the procedure at any time.
## Tips - Opening steps do some damage (incisions bleed, retractors bruise), so plan for that. - The hemostat heals a fixed amount per repeat, split across whatever damage the patient actually has. - Keep spare drapes around, each procedure uses one. - The operating table is the fastest surface in medbay. - Upgrade your tools when you can, experimental-tier gear with an operating table and surgical drape is roughly 4x faster than improvising.
